generatedSentence: A grizzled, noir lawyer with a fixed mindset, past his prime, found himself constantly picking at the edges of a truth so strange it felt like reality was warping. His current case involved an underground trade of remixed memories, a bizarre demonstration of psychological warfare. He battled an irrational hydrophobia, a mental bottleneck preventing him from fully grasping the data. His dexterity in navigating the city's underbelly was unmatched, a familiarity built from years of faceoff with shady characters. He was trying to count the times he'd encountered such nutjobs. The thought of the vampish vendor who supplied these illicit goods gave him lockjaw, a sense of dread that felt like a mental lobotomy. Even in his pyjamas, he was mentally piggybacking on old intel, feeling the truth slither closer, echoing the resilient mindset of desperate mothers. His investigation uncovered unbelievable subsystems designed to encrypt a radioactive enigma, hidden within an obscure filmography of abandoned data centers, replete with dusty furniture. Following a cryptic itinerary, he felt increasingly trapped in a digital maze of holography and surveillance, as if constantly hounded by digital paparazzi. He needed to outplay the architects of this system, which felt utterly fixed against him. He found peculiar robotic duckies acting as mobile listening posts, and the unclean data gave him pause. The challenge jazzes his resolve, even if it meant paying a heavy subscription for access or using his fists to break the lock on the keyways to truth. He was unhappy but kept volunteering his efforts, prepared to pursue leads via snowmobile through virtual hyperspace past whirring turbines. A sudden evacuation alarm choked the air, a cacophony echoing through the terminal as the temperature plummeted, signaling a nuclear tragedy. He felt a turgid wave of panic, but an old brawler's instinct, an anachronism in this digital age, surged. From a hidden lookout, he watched wicked racers on crawler vehicles attempting to unwrap encrypted files, their cannons poised. He quickly downed his cold coffee, stuffing earbuds into his ears to block the noise. He needed to get to the data ferry before the information overflow rendered everything useless. The ordinal sequence of events unfolded rapidly; the halcyon days of peace were truly over. He winked to himself, a grim acknowledgment of the impending chaos. He considered this a hard scholarship in survival, far from trivial, like trying to save a rotting watermelon by irrigated systems. The aftermath was a plethora of broken systems and simmering trauma. The shadowy racketeer, a notorious crook, had flunked his ultimate test, but not without leaving a devastating virus behind. The city's digital infrastructure was almost completely offline, its systems in a state of heresy and decay, like digital graveyards. The clean-up required un_locking knots of corrupted data with slow, deliberate viscosity. They had to eliminate the last remnants of the pillage. Even after being whipped into action, he found himself napping from exhaustion, trying to tackle the pervasive influence of online trolls who now claimed victory. The paramount task of washing away the technological fallout remained, far more complex than any game of soccer.
panelSentences: A grizzled, noir lawyer with a fixed mindset, past his prime, found himself constantly picking at the edges of a truth so strange it felt like reality was warping. His current case involved an underground trade of remixed memories, a bizarre demonstration of psychological warfare. He battled an irrational hydrophobia, a mental bottleneck preventing him from fully grasping the data. His dexterity in navigating the city's underbelly was unmatched, a familiarity built from years of faceoff with shady characters. He was trying to count the times he'd encountered such nutjobs. The thought of the vampish vendor who supplied these illicit goods gave him lockjaw, a sense of dread that felt like a mental lobotomy. Even in his pyjamas, he was mentally piggybacking on old intel, feeling the truth slither closer, echoing the resilient mindset of desperate mothers.

His investigation uncovered unbelievable subsystems designed to encrypt a radioactive enigma, hidden within an obscure filmography of abandoned data centers, replete with dusty furniture. Following a cryptic itinerary, he felt increasingly trapped in a digital maze of holography and surveillance, as if constantly hounded by digital paparazzi. He needed to outplay the architects of this system, which felt utterly fixed against him. He found peculiar robotic duckies acting as mobile listening posts, and the unclean data gave him pause. The challenge jazzes his resolve, even if it meant paying a heavy subscription for access or using his fists to break the lock on the keyways to truth. He was unhappy but kept volunteering his efforts, prepared to pursue leads via snowmobile through virtual hyperspace past whirring turbines.

A sudden evacuation alarm choked the air, a cacophony echoing through the terminal as the temperature plummeted, signaling a nuclear tragedy. He felt a turgid wave of panic, but an old brawler's instinct, an anachronism in this digital age, surged. From a hidden lookout, he watched wicked racers on crawler vehicles attempting to unwrap encrypted files, their cannons poised. He quickly downed his cold coffee, stuffing earbuds into his ears to block the noise. He needed to get to the data ferry before the information overflow rendered everything useless. The ordinal sequence of events unfolded rapidly; the halcyon days of peace were truly over. He winked to himself, a grim acknowledgment of the impending chaos. He considered this a hard scholarship in survival, far from trivial, like trying to save a rotting watermelon by irrigated systems.

The aftermath was a plethora of broken systems and simmering trauma. The shadowy racketeer, a notorious crook, had flunked his ultimate test, but not without leaving a devastating virus behind. The city's digital infrastructure was almost completely offline, its systems in a state of heresy and decay, like digital graveyards. The clean-up required un_locking knots of corrupted data with slow, deliberate viscosity. They had to eliminate the last remnants of the pillage. Even after being whipped into action, he found himself napping from exhaustion, trying to tackle the pervasive influence of online trolls who now claimed victory. The paramount task of washing away the technological fallout remained, far more complex than any game of soccer.
rawPanelSentences: A grizzled, **noir** **lawyer** with a **fixed mindset**, past his prime, found himself constantly **picking** at the edges of a truth so strange it felt like reality was **warping**. His current case involved an **underground trade** of **remixed** memories, a bizarre **demonstration** of psychological warfare. He battled an **irrational** **hydrophobia**, a mental **bottleneck** preventing him from fully grasping the data. His **dexterity** in navigating the city's underbelly was unmatched, a **familiarity** built from years of **faceoff** with shady characters. He was trying to **count** the times he'd encountered such **nutjobs**. The thought of the **vampish vendor** who supplied these illicit goods gave him **lockjaw**, a sense of dread that felt like a mental **lobotomy**. Even in his **pyjamas**, he was mentally **piggybacking** on old intel, feeling the truth **slither** closer, echoing the resilient **mindset** of desperate **mothers**.

His investigation uncovered **unbelievable** **subsystems** designed to **encrypt** a **radioactive** **enigma**, hidden within an **obscure** **filmography** of abandoned data centers, replete with dusty **furniture**. Following a cryptic **itinerary**, he felt increasingly **trapped** in a digital maze of **holography** and surveillance, as if constantly hounded by digital **paparazzi**. He needed to **outplay** the architects of this system, which felt utterly **fixed** against him. He found peculiar robotic **duckies** acting as mobile listening posts, and the **unclean** data gave him pause. The challenge **jazzes** his resolve, even if it meant paying a heavy **subscription** for access or using his **fists** to break the **lock** on the **keyways** to truth. He was **unhappy** but kept **volunteering** his efforts, prepared to pursue leads via **snowmobile** through virtual **hyperspace** past whirring **turbines**.

A sudden **evacuation** alarm **choked** the air, a cacophony **echoing** through the **terminal** as the **temperature** plummeted, signaling a **nuclear** **tragedy**. He felt a **turgid** wave of panic, but an old **brawler**'s instinct, an **anachronism** in this digital age, surged. From a hidden **lookout**, he watched **wicked** **racers** on **crawler** vehicles attempting to **unwrap** encrypted **files**, their **cannons** poised. He quickly downed his cold **coffee**, stuffing **earbuds** into his ears to block the noise. He needed to get to the data **ferry** before the information **overflow** rendered everything useless. The **ordinal** sequence of events unfolded rapidly; the **halcyon** days of peace were truly over. He **winked** to himself, a grim acknowledgment of the impending chaos. He considered this a hard **scholarship** in survival, far from **trivial**, like trying to save a rotting **watermelon** by **irrigated** systems.

The aftermath was a **plethora** of broken systems and simmering **trauma**. The shadowy **racketeer**, a notorious **crook**, had **flunked** his ultimate test, but not without leaving a devastating **virus** behind. The city's digital infrastructure was almost completely **offline**, its systems in a state of **heresy** and decay, like digital **graveyards**. The clean-up required **un_locking** **knots** of corrupted data with slow, deliberate **viscosity**. They had to **eliminate** the last remnants of the **pillage**. Even after being **whipped** into action, he found himself **napping** from exhaustion, trying to **tackle** the pervasive influence of online **trolls** who now claimed victory. The **paramount** task of **washing** away the technological fallout remained, far more complex than any game of **soccer**.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, dark noir graphic novel style, thick white gutters, clean black borders, cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. Protagonist is a weary, middle-aged male lawyer with deep-set eyes, a five-o'clock shadow, and a rumpled suit, showing visible facial wrinkles and graying temples. TOP-LEFT PANEL: A grizzled male lawyer sits at a cluttered desk in a dimly lit, rain-streaked office, scrutinizing a glowing, 'warping' digital screen filled with obscure data, close-up shot of his intense, mature face. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The same adult lawyer navigates a sprawling, underground server farm with towering, whirring turbines and holographic projections, a small, mechanical 'ducky' drone flying nearby, wide-angle shot from behind him. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The lawyer, amidst a chaotic 'evacuation' scene in a futuristic transportation terminal, dodges 'crawler' vehicles equipped with 'cannons', with a panicked crowd of adults in the background, dynamic medium shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The exhausted, older lawyer sits slumped against a wall in a ruined, 'offline' cityscape, gazing at a distant, digitally 'corrupted' skyline, reflecting on the 'trauma', low-angle shot.

definitions.definition: To twist or become twisted, physically or mentally:
(ropemaking) To run (yarn) off the reel into hauls to be tarred.
To arrange (strands of thread, etc) so that they run lengthwise in weaving.
To plot; to fabricate or weave (a plot or scheme).
To change or fix (make fixed, for example by freezing).
To move:
(obsolete outside dialectal, of an animal) To bring forth (young) prematurely.
To fertilize (low-lying land) by letting the tide, a river, or other water in upon it to deposit silt and alluvial matter.
(very rare) To throw.
An action or motion that warps or twists.
The deformation of the Earth's crust over a large area
The art or occupation of preparing warp or webs for the weaver.
The practice of flooding agricultural land with turbid river water to add sediment to the soil.
The act of counting or tallying a quantity.
definitions.example: Give the chairs a quick count to check if we have enough.

definitions.definition: The result of a tally that reveals the number of items in a set; a quantity counted.
A countdown.
A charge of misconduct brought in a legal proceeding.
The number of balls and strikes, respectively, on a batter's in-progress plate appearance.
definitions.example: He has a 3–2 count with the bases loaded.

definitions.definition: An object of interest or account; value; estimation.
To recite numbers in sequence.
definitions.example: Can you count to a hundred?

definitions.definition: To determine the number (of objects in a group).
definitions.example: There are three apples; count them.

definitions.definition: To be of significance; to matter.
definitions.example: Your views don’t count here.    It does count if you cheat with someone when you’re drunk.

definitions.definition: To be an example of something: often followed by as and an indefinite noun.
definitions.example: Apples count as a type of fruit.

definitions.definition: To consider something an example of something.
definitions.example: He counts himself a hero after saving the cat from the river.   I count you as more than a friend.

definitions.definition: To take account or note (of).
To plead orally; to argue a matter in court; to recite a count.
(grammar) Countable.
Clothes for wearing to bed and sleeping in, usually consisting of a loose-fitting shirt and pants/trousers.
Loose-fitting trousers worn by both sexes in various southern Asian countries including India.
To attach or append something to another (usually larger) object or event.
definitions.example: The popular host can’t claim credit for the trade, though. The idea wasn’t his. He piggybacked off another successful investor who had a history of picking winners.

definitions.definition: To obtain a wireless internet connection by bringing one's own computer within the range of another's wireless connection without that subscriber's permission or knowledge.
To utilize "last-mile" wiring rented from a larger owner ISP by a smaller ISP.
To carry someone on the back or shoulders.
To transport (a lorry/truck) on a flatbed railway waggon
To enter a secured area at the same time along with someone having authorized access; to tailgate
Skill in performing tasks, especially with the hands.
definitions.example: Playing computer games can improve your manual dexterity.

definitions.definition: A limestone rubble.
(see usage notes) A sliver.
To move about smoothly and from side to side.
To slide
Slithery; slippery
The state of being extremely friendly; intimacy.
Undue intimacy; inappropriate informality, impertinence.
An instance of familiar behaviour.
Close or habitual acquaintance with someone or something; understanding or recognition acquired from experience.
The method used to begin play in ice hockey.
A real number that can not be expressed as the quotient of two integers, an irrational number.
Not rational; unfounded or nonsensical.
definitions.example: an irrational decision

definitions.definition: Of a real number, that cannot be written as the ratio of two integers.
definitions.example: The number π is irrational.

definitions.definition: The narrow portion that forms the pouring spout of a bottle; the neck of a bottle.
In traffic, any narrowing of the road, especially resulting in a delay.
(by extension) The part of a process that is too slow or cumbersome.
definitions.example: It is easy to create entries; processing the paperwork is the bottleneck.

definitions.definition: To slow by causing a bottleneck.
definitions.example: The merge bottlenecked the traffic every morning.

definitions.definition: To form a bottleneck.
definitions.example: The traffic bottlenecked at the merge every morning.

definitions.definition: Someone who is crazy (insane).
definitions.example: Some nut job from the government actually thought he could tax our moonshine!

definitions.definition: Film noir.
A production in the style of film noir.
Of or pertaining to film noir, or the atmosphere associated with that genre
Buying and selling of goods and services on a market.
A particular instance of buying or selling.
definitions.example: I did no trades with them once the rumors started.

definitions.definition: An instance of bartering items in exchange for one another.
Those who perform a particular kind of skilled work.
definitions.example: The skilled trades were the first to organize modern labor unions.

definitions.definition: Those engaged in an industry or group of related industries.
definitions.example: It is not a retail showroom. It is only for the trade.

definitions.definition: The skilled practice of a practical occupation.
definitions.example: He learned his trade as an apprentice.

definitions.definition: An occupation in the secondary sector, as opposed to an agricultural, professional or military one.
definitions.example: After failing his entrance exams, he decided to go into a trade.

definitions.definition: The business given to a commercial establishment by its customers.
definitions.example: Even before noon there was considerable trade.

definitions.definition: (chiefly in the plural) Steady winds blowing from east to west above and below the equator.
definitions.example: They rode the trades going west.

definitions.definition: (only as plural) A publication intended for participants in an industry or related group of industries.
definitions.example: Rumors about layoffs are all over the trades.

definitions.definition: A brief sexual encounter.
definitions.example: Josh picked up some trade last night.

definitions.definition: Instruments of any occupation.
Refuse or rubbish from a mine.
A track or trail; a way; a path; passage.
Course; custom; practice; occupation.
To engage in trade.
definitions.example: This company trades (in) precious metal.

definitions.definition: To be traded at a certain price or under certain conditions.
definitions.example: Apple is trading at $200.

definitions.definition: To give (something) in exchange for.
definitions.example: Will you trade your precious watch for my earring?

definitions.definition: To give someone a plant and receive a different one in return.
To do business; offer for sale as for one's livelihood.
To have dealings; to be concerned or associated (with).
To mix again.
To create a remix.
To rearrange or radically alter (a particular piece of music).
To modify (a work in any medium).
An aversion to water, as a symptom of rabies; the disease of rabies itself.
A morbid fear of water; aquaphobia.
An underground railway, especially for mass transit of people in urban areas.
A train that runs on such an underground railway.
A rapid transit system, regardless of the elevation of its right of way.
An underground walkway, tunnel for pedestrians (called pedestrian underpass in US).
An underground route for pipes, sewers, etc.
Regions beneath the surface of the earth, both natural (eg. caves) and man-made (eg. mines).
(with definite article) A movement or organisation of people who resist political convention.
definitions.example: the French underground during World War II

definitions.definition: (with definite article) A movement or organisation of people who resist artistic convention.
To route electricity distribution cables underground
Below the ground; below the surface of the Earth.
definitions.example: There is an underground tunnel that takes you across the river.

definitions.definition: Hidden, furtive, secretive.
definitions.example: These criminals operate through an underground network.

definitions.definition: Outside the mainstream, especially unofficial and hidden from the authorities.
definitions.example: underground music

definitions.definition: Below the ground.
definitions.example: The tunnel goes underground at this point.

definitions.definition: Secretly.
Like a vamp (flirtatious woman).
A person or a company that vends or sells.
A vending machine.
To bundle third-party dependencies with the source code for one's own program.
definitions.example: I distributed my application with a vendored copy of Perl so that it wouldn't use the system copies of Perl where it is installed.

definitions.definition: As the software vendor, to bundle one's own, possibly modified version of dependencies with a standard program.
definitions.example: Strawberry Perl contains vendored copies of some CPAN modules, designed to allow them to run on Windows.

definitions.definition: A person (especially an entomologist) or animal that catches moths.
A (human) female who has given birth to a baby
definitions.example: He had something of his mother in him.

definitions.definition: A human female who parents an adopted or fostered child
A human female who donates a fertilized egg or donates a body cell which has resulted in a clone.
A pregnant female, possibly as a shortened form of mother-to-be.
definitions.example: Nutrients and oxygen obtained by the mother are conveyed to the fetus.

definitions.definition: A female parent of an animal.
definitions.example: The lioness was a mother of four cubs.

definitions.definition: A female ancestor.
A source or origin.
definitions.example: The Mediterranean was mother to many cultures and languages.

definitions.definition: Something that is the greatest or most significant of its kind. (See mother of all.)
(when followed by a surname) A title of respect for one's mother-in-law.
definitions.example: Mother Smith, meet my cousin, Doug Jones.

definitions.definition: Any elderly woman, especially within a particular community.
Any person or entity which performs mothering.
The principal piece of an astrolabe, into which the others are fixed.
The female superior or head of a religious house; an abbess, etc.
Hysterical passion; hysteria; the uterus.
To give birth to or produce (as its female parent) a child. (Compare father.)
To treat as a mother would be expected to treat her child; to nurture.

A stringy, mucilaginous or film- or membrane-like substance (consisting of acetobacters) which develops in fermenting alcoholic liquids (such as wine, or cider), and turns the alcohol into acetic acid with the help of oxygen from the air.
definitions.example: pieces of mother, adding mother to vinegar

definitions.definition: To cause to contain mother.
definitions.example: mothered oil / vinegar / wine

definitions.definition: (of an alcohol) To develop mother.
Motherfucker.
A striking example.
A way of thinking; an attitude or opinion, especially a habitual one.
definitions.example: Earth Day is a way of propagating and celebrating the environmentalist mindset.

definitions.definition: A professional person qualified (as by a law degree or bar exam) and authorized to practice law, i.e. represent parties in lawsuits or trials and give legal advice.
definitions.example: A lawyer's time and advice are his stock in trade. - aphorism often credited to Abraham Lincoln, but without attestation

definitions.definition: (by extension) A legal layman who argues points of law.
The burbot.
The stem of a bramble.
To practice law.
To perform, or attempt to perform, the work of a lawyer.
To make legalistic arguments.
To barrage (a person) with questions in order to get them to admit something.
definitions.example: You've been lawyered!

definitions.definition: A spasmodic, nervous system disease brought on by the tetanus bacteria. It causes muscles to seize up and may cause death by suffocation.
A surgical operation on the frontal lobe of the brain intent on treating certain mental illnesses.
The severing of the prefrontal cortex from the thalamic region of the brain.
The severing of the sympathetic nerve trunk.
To grasp and pull with the fingers or fingernails.
definitions.example: Don't pick at that scab.

definitions.definition: To harvest a fruit or vegetable for consumption by removing it from the plant to which it is attached; to harvest an entire plant by removing it from the ground.
definitions.example: It's time to pick the tomatoes.

definitions.definition: To pull apart or away, especially with the fingers; to pluck.
definitions.example: She picked flowers in the meadow.

definitions.definition: To take up; especially, to gather from here and there; to collect; to bring together.
definitions.example: to pick rags

definitions.definition: To remove something from somewhere with a pointed instrument, with the fingers, or with the teeth.
definitions.example: to pick the teeth; to pick a bone; to pick a goose; to pick a pocket

definitions.definition: To decide upon, from a set of options; to select.
definitions.example: I'll pick the one with the nicest name.

definitions.definition: To seek (a fight or quarrel) where the opportunity arises.
To recognise the type of ball being bowled by a bowler by studying the position of the hand and arm as the ball is released.
definitions.example: He didn't pick the googly, and was bowled.

definitions.definition: To pluck the individual strings of a musical instrument or to play such an instrument.
definitions.example: He picked a tune on his banjo.

definitions.definition: To open (a lock) with a wire, lock pick, etc.
To eat slowly, sparingly, or by morsels; to nibble.
To do anything fastidiously or carefully, or by attending to small things; to select something with care.
definitions.example: I gingerly picked my way between the thorny shrubs.

definitions.definition: To steal; to pilfer.
To throw; to pitch.
To peck at, as a bird with its beak; to strike at with anything pointed; to act upon with a pointed instrument; to pierce; to prick, as with a pin.
To separate or open by means of a sharp point or points.
definitions.example: to pick matted wool, cotton, oakum, etc.

definitions.definition: To screen.
A gathering to pick fruit.
definitions.example: We went to a strawberry picking last June.

definitions.definition: (usually pluralized) Items remaining after others have selected the best; scraps, as of food.
(usually pluralized) Income or other gains, especially if obtained in an unscrupulous or objectionable manner.
Something picked or pulled out.
definitions.example: The schoolboy flicked his nose pickings across the classroom.

definitions.definition: The act of making a choice; selection.
The final finishing of woven fabrics by removing burs, etc.
The removal of defects from electrotype plates.
Dabbing in stoneworking.
The act of demonstrating; showing or explaining something.
An event at which something will be demonstrated.
definitions.example: I have to give a demonstration to the class tomorrow, and I'm ill-prepared.

definitions.definition: Expression of one's feelings by outward signs.
A public display of group opinion, such as a protest march.
A show of military force.
A mathematical proof.
A duck (aquatic bird), especially a toy rubber duck
An affectionate pet name.
definitions.example: "Morning, ducky!" said Roderick, as he gave his partner a quick peck on the cheek and sat down to breakfast.

definitions.definition: A group of related components that are part of a larger system.
To pierce; now generally replaced by transfix.
To attach; to affix; to hold in place or at a particular time.
definitions.example: A dab of chewing gum will fix your note to the bulletin board.

definitions.definition: To mend, to repair.
definitions.example: That heater will start a fire if you don't fix it.

definitions.definition: To prepare (food or drink).
definitions.example: She fixed dinner for the kids.

definitions.definition: To make (a contest, vote, or gamble) unfair; to privilege one contestant or a particular group of contestants, usually before the contest begins; to arrange immunity for defendants by tampering with the justice system via bribery or extortion
definitions.example: A majority of voters believed the election was fixed in favor of the incumbent.

definitions.definition: To surgically render an animal, especially a pet, infertile.
definitions.example: Rover stopped digging under the fence after we had the vet fix him.

definitions.definition: (sematics) To map a (point or subset) to itself.
To take revenge on, to best; to serve justice on an assumed miscreant.
definitions.example: He got caught breaking into lockers, so a couple of guys fixed him after work.

definitions.definition: To render (a photographic impression) permanent by treating with such applications as will make it insensitive to the action of light.
To convert into a stable or available form.
definitions.example: Legumes are valued in crop rotation for their ability to fix nitrogen.

definitions.definition: To become fixed; to settle or remain permanently; to cease from wandering; to rest.
To become firm, so as to resist volatilization; to cease to flow or be fluid; to congeal; to become hard and malleable, as a metallic substance.
Not changing, not able to be changed, staying the same.
definitions.example: Every religion has its own fixed ideas.

definitions.definition: Stationary.
Attached; affixed.
Chemically stable.
Supplied with what one needs.
definitions.example: She's nicely fixed after two divorce settlements.

definitions.definition: Of sound, recorded on a permanent medium.
definitions.example: In the United States, recordings are only granted copyright protection when the sounds in the recording were fixed and first published on or after February 15, 1972.

definitions.definition: Surgically rendered infertile (spayed, neutered or castrated).
definitions.example: a fixed tomcat; the she-cat has been fixed

definitions.definition: Rigged; fraudulently prearranged.
(of a problem) Resolved; corrected.
Repaired
A selective list of movie titles that share a similar characteristic such as the same genre, the same director, the same actor etc.
Large movable item(s), usually in a room, which enhance(s) the room's characteristics, functionally or decoratively.
definitions.example: A chair is furniture. Sofas are also furniture.

definitions.definition: The harness, trappings etc. of a horse, hawk, or other animal.
Fittings, such as handles, of a door, coffin, or other wooden item.
The stock and forearm of a weapon.
The pieces of wood or metal put round pages of type to make proper margins and fill the spaces between the pages and the chase.
Any material on the page other than the text and pictures of stories.
To render obscure; to darken; to make dim; to keep in the dark; to hide; to make less visible, intelligible, legible, glorious, beautiful, or illustrious.
To hide, put out of sight etc.
To conceal oneself; to hide.
Dark, faint or indistinct.
Hidden, out of sight or inconspicuous.
Difficult to understand.
definitions.example: an obscure passage or inscription;    The speaker made obscure references to little-known literary works.

definitions.definition: Not well-known.
Unknown or uncertain; unclear.
definitions.example: The etymological roots of the word "blizzard" are obscure and open to debate.

definitions.definition: To conceal information by means of a code or cipher.
A written schedule of activities for a vacation or road trip.
A route or proposed route of a journey.
An account or record of a journey.
A guidebook for travellers.
Itinerant; travelling from place to place; done on a journey
Any radioactive substance.
Exhibiting radioactivity.
Dangerous and disgusting, particularly of people or ideas.
definitions.example: Even sleazy tactics and his radioactive mouth may not be able to contain this debacle.

definitions.definition: An n-dimensional Euclidian space with n > 3.
A Euclidian space of unspecified dimension.
A notional space orthogonal to the usual dimensions of space-time often used for faster-than-light travel.
definitions.example: We can only get to the Nimrodian Galaxy if we go through hyperspace.

definitions.definition: To travel or transport into hyperspace.
Not to be believed.
Incredible; so surprising it is almost unable to believe.
definitions.example: The most unbelievable thing happened to me today!

definitions.definition: Implausible or improbable.
definitions.example: His excuse seems rather unbelievable.

definitions.definition: Dirty, soiled or foul.
Not moral or chaste.
Ritually or ceremonially impure or unfit.
A slot into which a precision attachment (a key) is fitted.
Something used for fastening, which can only be opened with a key or combination.
(by extension) A mutex or other token restricting access to a resource.
A segment of a canal or other waterway enclosed by gates, used for raising and lowering boats between levels.
The firing mechanism.
Complete control over a situation.
Something sure to be a success.
A player in the scrum behind the front row, usually the tallest members of the team.
A fastening together or interlacing; a closing of one thing upon another; a state of being fixed or immovable.
A place from which egress is prevented, as by a lock.
A device for keeping a wheel from turning.
A grapple in wrestling.
To become fastened in place.

definitions.definition: To fasten with a lock.

definitions.definition: To be capable of becoming fastened in place.

definitions.definition: To intertwine or dovetail.

definitions.definition: (break dancing) To freeze one's body or a part thereof in place.

definitions.definition: To furnish (a canal) with locks.
To raise or lower (a boat) in a lock.
To seize (e.g. the sword arm of an antagonist) by turning the left arm around it, to disarm him.
To modify (a thread) so that users cannot make new posts in it.
(WMF jargon) To prevent a page from being edited by other users.
definitions.example: Frequently vandalized pages are generally locked to prevent further damage.

definitions.definition: A paparazzo.
Paparazzi taken as a group.
A vehicle with skis at the front and a caterpillar track at the rear, used for travelling over snow, sometimes as sport
To ride or race in a snowmobile.
Access to a resource for a period of time, generally for payment.
definitions.example: I have a monthly subscription to the Daily Telegraph newspaper.

definitions.definition: The formal acceptance of something, especially when verified with a signature.
The signing of one's name.
A hand with the fingers clenched or curled inward.
definitions.example: The boxer's fists rained down on his opponent in the last round.

definitions.definition: The pointing hand symbol ☞.
The characteristic signaling rhythm of an individual telegraph or CW operator when sending Morse code.
A person's characteristic handwriting.
A group of men.
The talons of a bird of prey.
An attempt at something.
To strike with the fist.
definitions.example: ...may not score a point with his open hand(s), but may score a point by fisting the ball. Damian Cullen. "Running the rule." The Irish Times 18 Aug 2003, pg. 52.

definitions.definition: To close (the hand) into a fist.
To grip with a fist.
To fist-fuck.
The act of breaking wind; fise.
A puffball.
To break wind.
To excel or defeat in a game; to play better than.
definitions.example: We were outplayed at tennis, but we outplayed them at football.

definitions.definition: To destroy.
definitions.example: You’ve gone and jazzed it now!

definitions.definition: To play (jazz music).
To dance to the tunes of jazz music.
To enliven, brighten up, make more colourful or exciting; excite
To complicate.
definitions.example: Don’t jazz it too much!

definitions.definition: To have sex for money, to prostitute oneself.
To move (around/about) in a lively or frivolous manner; to fool around.
To distract or pester.
definitions.example: Stop jazzing me!

definitions.definition: To ejaculate.
Something or someone puzzling, mysterious or inexplicable.
A riddle, or a difficult problem.
Any of various rotary machines that use the kinetic energy of a continuous stream of fluid (a liquid or a gas) to turn a shaft.
To physically capture, to catch in a trap or traps, or something like a trap.

definitions.definition: To ensnare; to take by stratagem; to entrap.
To provide with a trap.

definitions.definition: To set traps for game; to make a business of trapping game

definitions.definition: To leave suddenly, to flee.
To sell illegal drugs, especially in a public area.
To capture (e.g. an error) in order to handle or process it.
To attend to and open and close a (trap-)door.
To dress with ornaments; to adorn (especially said of horses).
Caught in a trap
A technique for recording, and then reconstructing, the amplitude and phase distributions of a coherent wave disturbance; used to produce three-dimensional images or holograms
An individual who is not happy.
Not happy; sad.
Not satisfied; unsatisfied.

definitions.definition: Not lucky; unlucky.
definitions.example: The doomed lovers must have been born under an unhappy star.

definitions.definition: Not suitable; unsuitable.
To enlist oneself as a volunteer.
To do or offer to do something voluntarily.

definitions.definition: To offer, usually unprompted.

definitions.definition: To grow without human sowing or intentional cultivation.
To offer the services of (someone else) to do something.

definitions.definition: The act of volunteering; the action of the verb volunteer
A beverage made by infusing the beans of the coffee plant in hot water.
A serving of this beverage.
The seeds of the plant used to make coffee, misnamed ‘beans’ due to their shape.
The powder made by roasting and grinding the seeds.
A tropical plant of the genus Coffea.
A pale brown colour, like that of milk coffee.
The end of a meal, when coffee is served.

definitions.definition: To drink coffee.
Of a pale brown colour, like that of milk coffee.
Of a table: a small, low table suitable for people in lounge seating to put coffee cups on.
A grant-in-aid to a student.
The character or qualities of a scholar.
The activity, methods or attainments of a scholar.
The sum of knowledge accrued by scholars; the realm of refined learning.
The first year of high school, often accompanied by exams that needed to be passed before advancement to the higher grades.
To attend an institution on a scholarship.
To grant a scholarship to.
A collection of papers collated and archived together.
A roll or list.
Course of thought; thread of narration.
An aggregation of data on a storage device, identified by a name.

definitions.definition: To commit (official papers) to some office.
To place in an archive in a logical place and order
To store a file (aggregation of data) on a storage medium such as a disc or another computer.
(with for) To submit a formal request to some office.
definitions.example: She filed for divorce the next day.

definitions.definition: To set in order; to arrange, or lay away.
A column of people one behind another, whether "single file" or in a large group with many files side by side.
definitions.example: The troops marched in Indian file.

definitions.definition: A small detachment of soldiers.
One of the eight vertical lines of squares on a chessboard (i.e., those identified by a letter). The analog horizontal lines are the ranks.
To move in a file.
definitions.example: The applicants kept filing into the room until it was full.

definitions.definition: A hand tool consisting of a handle to which a block of coarse metal is attached, and used for removing sharp edges or for cutting, especially through metal.
A cunning or resourceful person.
To smooth, grind, or cut with a file.
definitions.example: I'd better file the bottoms of the table legs. Otherwise they will scratch the flooring.

definitions.definition: To defile
To corrupt
The act of evacuating; leaving a place in an orderly fashion, especially for safety.
Withdrawal of troops or civils from a town, fortress, etc.
The act of emptying, clearing of the contents, or discharging, including creating a vacuum.
Voidance of any matter by the natural passages of the body or by an artificial opening; defecation; also, a diminution of the fluids of an animal body by cathartics, venesection, or other means.
That which is evacuated or discharged; especially, a discharge by stool or other natural means.
Abolition; nullification.
To supply (farmland) with water, by building ditches, pipes, etc.

definitions.definition: To clean (a wound) with a fluid.
definitions.example: The nurse will show you how to irrigate the wound to prevent infection.

definitions.definition: Nuclear power
Pertaining to the nucleus of an atom.
Involving energy released by nuclear reactions (fission, fusion, radioactive decay).
definitions.example: a nuclear reactor

definitions.definition: Relating to a weapon that derives its force from rapid release of energy through nuclear reactions.
definitions.example: a nuclear explosion

definitions.definition: (by extension, metaphoric, of a solution or response) Involving an extreme course of action.
definitions.example: nuclear option, nuclear solution

definitions.definition: Pertaining to the nucleus of a cell.
Pertaining to a centre around which something is developed or organised; central, pivotal.
A drama or similar work, in which the main character is brought to ruin or otherwise suffers the extreme consequences of some tragic flaw or weakness of character.
The genre of such works, and the art of producing them.
A disastrous event, especially one involving great loss of life or injury.
Someone who takes part in a race.
A racehorse.
An animal known for its fast speed, or suitable for racing; applied especially to a number of North American snakes, certain kinds of lake trout, etc.
A vehicle or other device used for racing.
Any of several genera of colubrid snakes.
A video game involving racing of any kind.
A kingfisher said in classical mythology to nest on the sea, thereby calming the waters.
A tropical kingfisher of the genus Halcyon, such as the sacred kingfisher (Halcyon sancta) of Australia.
Pertaining to the halcyon or kingfisher.
Calm, undisturbed, peaceful, serene.
People who are wicked.
Evil or mischievous by nature.
Excellent; awesome; masterful.

definitions.definition: Very, extremely.
definitions.example: The band we went to see the other night was wicked loud!

definitions.definition: A person who is abused, physically or verbally, and returns to the abuser a supplicant.
A sycophant.
A complete assembly, consisting of an artillery tube and a breech mechanism, firing mechanism or base cap, which is a component of a gun, howitzer or mortar. It may include muzzle appendages.
Any similar device for shooting material out of a tube.
A bone of a horse's leg, between the fetlock joint and the knee or hock.
A cannon bit.
A large muzzle-loading artillery piece.
A carom.
definitions.example: In English billiards, a cannon is when one's cue ball strikes the other player's cue ball and the red ball on the same shot; and it is worth two points.

definitions.definition: The arm of a player that can throw well.
definitions.example: He's got a cannon out in right.

definitions.definition: A hollow cylindrical piece carried by a revolving shaft, on which it may, however, revolve independently.
(Chinese chess) A piece which moves horizontally and vertically like a rook but captures another piece by jumping over a different piece in the line of attack.
To bombard with cannons.
To play the carom billiard shot. To strike two balls with the cue ball
definitions.example: The white cannoned off the red onto the pink.

definitions.definition: To fire something, especially spherical, rapidly.
To collide or strike violently, especially so as to glance off or rebound.
A generally accepted principle; a rule.

definitions.definition: A group of literary works that are generally accepted as representing a field.
The works of a writer that have been accepted as authentic.

definitions.definition: A eucharistic prayer, particularly the Roman Canon.
A religious law or body of law decreed by the church.

definitions.definition: A catalogue of saints acknowledged and canonized in the Roman Catholic Church.
In monasteries, a book containing the rules of a religious order.
A member of a cathedral chapter; one who possesses a prebend in a cathedral or collegiate church.
A piece of music in which the same melody is played by different voices, but beginning at different times; a round.
definitions.example: Pachelbel’s Canon has become very popular.

definitions.definition: (Roman law) A rent or stipend payable at some regular time, generally annual, e.g., canon frumentarius
Those sources, especially including literary works, which are considered part of the main continuity regarding a given fictional universe.
definitions.example: A spin-off book series revealed the aliens to be originally from Earth, but it's not canon.

definitions.definition: A rolled and filleted loin of meat; also called cannon.
definitions.example: a canon of beef or lamb

definitions.definition: A large size of type formerly used for printing the church canons, standardized as 48-point.
The part of a bell by which it is suspended; the ear or shank of a bell.
A carom.
A clergy member serving a cathedral or collegiate church.
A canon regular, a member of any of several Roman Catholic religious orders.
A Near Eastern and Caucasian musical instrument related to the zither, dulcimer, or harp having either 26 strings and a single bridge, or twice that number and two bridges.
A ship used to transport people, smaller vehicles and goods from one port to another, usually on a regular schedule.
A place where passengers are transported across water in such a ship.
The legal right or franchise that entitles a corporate body or an individual to operate such a service.
To carry; transport; convey.
definitions.example: Trucks plowed through the water to ferry flood victims to safety.

definitions.definition: To move someone or something from one place to another, usually repeatedly.
definitions.example: Being a good waiter takes more than the ability to ferry plates of food around a restaurant.

definitions.definition: To carry or transport over a contracted body of water, as a river or strait, in a boat or other floating conveyance plying between opposite shores.
To pass over water in a boat or by ferry.
The spillage resultant from overflow; excess.
Outlet for escape of excess material.
The situation where a value exceeds the available numeric range.
To flow over the brim of (a container).

definitions.definition: To cover with a liquid, literally or figuratively.

definitions.definition: To cause an overflow.
To flow over the edge of a container.

definitions.definition: To exceed limits or capacity.

definitions.definition: To be superabundant; to abound.
(of a sound or sound waves) To reflect off a surface and return.
To reflect back (a sound).
(by extension) To repeat (another's speech, opinion etc.).

definitions.definition: To repeat its input as input to some other device or system.

An immature ear of maize from which the threadlike silks emerge.
An underdeveloped mammalian ear.
A small earphone designed for use with portable sound systems.
A cotton swab.
One who brawls, engages in noisy, unseemly fights.
A beat 'em up game.
Any of the three liberal arts forming the trivium.
Ignorable; of little significance or value.
Commonplace, ordinary.
Concerned with or involving trivia.
Relating to or designating the name of a species; specific as opposed to generic.
Of, relating to, or being the simplest possible case.
Self-evident.
Pertaining to the trivium.
Indistinguishable in case of truth or falsity.
A chronological mistake; the erroneous dating of an event, circumstance, or object.
A person or thing which seems to belong to a different time or period of time.
To open or undo, as what is wrapped or folded.
To become unwrapped.
To remove word wrap from.
A vantage point with a view of the surrounding area.
A session of watching for an approaching enemy, police, etc.

definitions.definition: A person on watch for approaching enemy, police, danger, etc.
definitions.example: The raid failed when the lookout noticed the enemy group.

definitions.definition: A subject for observation; a prospect or view.
One's perspective, outlook; hence, one's responsibility. (used with a possessive pronoun or a noun in a possessive form).
definitions.example: Every man's interest is his own lookout.

definitions.definition: A plant of the species Citrullus lanatus, bearing a melon-like fruit.
The fruit of the watermelon plant, having a green rind and watery flesh that is typically bright red when ripe and contains black pips.
An environmentalist with socialist leanings (from the similarity to the fruit, being green on the outside, and red (Communist) on the inside).
A pinkish-red colour, like that of watermelon flesh (also called watermelon pink).
To close one's eyes in sleep.
To close one's eyes.
Usually followed by at: to look the other way, to turn a blind eye.
To close one's eyes quickly and involuntarily; to blink.
To blink with only one eye as a message, signal, or suggestion, usually with an implication of conspiracy. (When transitive, the object may be the eye being winked, or the message being conveyed.)
definitions.example: He winked at me. She winked her eye. He winked his assent.

definitions.definition: To gleam fitfully or intermitently; to twinkle; to flicker.
To be unable to breathe because of obstruction of the windpipe (for instance food or other objects that go down the wrong way, or fumes or particles in the air that cause the throat to constrict).
definitions.example: Ever since he choked on a bone, he has refused to eat fish.

definitions.definition: To prevent (someone) from breathing or talking by strangling or filling the windpipe.

definitions.definition: To obstruct (a passage, etc.) by filling it up or clogging it.
definitions.example: to choke a cave passage with boulders and mud

definitions.definition: To hinder or check, as growth, expansion, progress, etc.; to kill (a plant by robbing it of nutrients); to extinguish (fire by robbing it of oxygen).
To perform badly at a crucial stage of a competition because one is nervous, especially when one is winning.
definitions.example: He has a lot of talent, but he tends to choke under pressure.

definitions.definition: To move one's fingers very close to the tip of a pencil, brush or other art tool.
To be checked or stopped, as if by choking
To check or stop (an utterance or voice) as if by choking.
To have a feeling of strangulation in one's throat as a result of passion or strong emotion.
To give (someone) a feeling of strangulation as a result of passion or strong emotion.
To say (something) with one’s throat constricted (due to emotion, for example).
To use the choke valve of (a vehicle) to adjust the air/fuel mixture in the engine.
(of a duct) To reach a condition of maximum flowrate, due to the flow at the narrowest point of the duct becoming sonic (Ma = 1).
To make a choke, as in a cartridge, or in the bore of the barrel of a shotgun.
Having been unable to breathe due to airway obstruction (choking) or strangulation, but usually to the point of pain and discomfort without death.
Blocked or obstructed by thick material, often plant growth.
An ordinal number such as first, second and third.
definitions.example: The most common numerals in Latin, as in English, are the "cardinals"...and the "ordinals"... — F. M. Wheelock, Wheelock’s Latin, 6th ed. revised (2005), p97

definitions.definition: A book used in the ordination of Anglican ministers, or in certain Roman Catholic services
(of a number) indicating position in a sequence
Of or relating to the groupings called orders
Intercardinal
Distended beyond the natural state by some internal agent, especially fluid, or expansive force.
definitions.example: I have a turgid limb.

definitions.definition: (of language or style) Overly complex and difficult to understand; grandiloquent; bombastic.
A building in an airport where passengers transfer from ground transportation to the facilities that allow them to board airplanes.
A harbour facility where ferries embark and disembark passengers and load and unload vehicles.
A rail station where service begins and ends; the end of the line. For example: Grand Central Terminal in New York City.

A rate charged on all freight, regardless of distance, and supposed to cover the expenses of station service, as distinct from mileage rate, generally proportionate to the distance and intended to cover movement expenses.
A town lying at the end of a railroad, in which the terminal is located; more properly called a terminus.
A storage tank for bulk liquids (such as oil or chemicals) prior to further distribution.
The end of a line where signals are either transmitted or received, or a point along the length of a line where the signals are made available to apparatus.
An electric contact on a battery.
The apparatus to send and/or receive signals on a line, such as a telephone or network device.
A device for entering data into a computer or a communications system and/or displaying data received, especially a device equipped with a keyboard and some sort of textual display.
A computer program that emulates a physical terminal.
A terminal symbol in a formal grammar.
The end ramification (of an axon, etc.) or one of the extremities of a polypeptid.
To store bulk liquids (such as oil or chemicals) in storage tanks prior to further distribution.
Fatal; resulting in death.

definitions.definition: Appearing at the end; top or apex of a physical object.
Occurring at the end of a word, sentence, or period of time, and serves to terminate it
Occurring every term; termly.
definitions.example: a student's terminal fees

definitions.definition: A measure of cold or heat, often measurable with a thermometer.
definitions.example: The temperature in the room dropped nearly 20 degrees; it went from hot to cold.

definitions.definition: An elevated body temperature, as present in fever and many illnesses.
definitions.example: You have a temperature. I think you should stay home today. You’re sick.

definitions.definition: A property of macroscopic amounts of matter that serves to gauge the average intensity of the random actual motions of the individually mobile particulate constituents. http//arxiv.org/pdf/physics/0004055
The state or condition of being tempered or moderated.
The balance of humours in the body, or one's character or outlook as considered determined from this; temperament.
One who commits crimes (especially fraud, bribery, loansharking, extortion etc.) to aid in running a shady or illegal business.
One who instigates or has involvement with a racket.
To carry out illegal business activities or criminal schemes.
To commit crimes systematically as part of a criminal organization.
The state of being viscous.
A quantity expressing the magnitude of internal friction in a fluid, as measured by the force per unit area resisting uniform flow.
A tendency to prolong interpersonal encounters.
A looping of a piece of string or of any other long, flexible material that cannot be untangled without passing one or both ends of the material through its loops.
definitions.example: Climbers must make sure that all knots are both secure and of types that will not weaken the rope.

definitions.definition: (of hair, etc) A tangled clump.
definitions.example: The nurse was brushing knots from the protesting child's hair.

definitions.definition: A maze-like pattern.
A non-self-intersecting closed curve in (e.g., three-dimensional) space that is an abstraction of a knot (in sense 1 above).
definitions.example: A knot can be defined as a non-self-intersecting broken line whose endpoints coincide: when such a knot is constrained to lie in a plane, then it is simply a polygon.

definitions.definition: A difficult situation.
definitions.example: I got into a knot when I inadvertently insulted a policeman.

definitions.definition: The whorl left in lumber by the base of a branch growing out of the tree's trunk.
definitions.example: When preparing to tell stories at a campfire, I like to set aside a pile of pine logs with lots of knots, since they burn brighter and make dramatic pops and cracks.

definitions.definition: Local swelling in a tissue area, especially skin, often due to injury.
definitions.example: Jeremy had a knot on his head where he had bumped it on the bedframe.

definitions.definition: A protuberant joint in a plant.
Any knob, lump, swelling, or protuberance.
The swelling of the bulbus glandis in members of the dog family, Canidae
The point on which the action of a story depends; the gist of a matter.
definitions.example: the knot of the tale

definitions.definition: A node.
A kind of epaulet; a shoulder knot.
A group of people or things.
A bond of union; a connection; a tie.

A unit of speed, equal to one nautical mile per hour. (From the practice of counting the number of knots in the log-line (as it is paid out) in a standard time. Traditionally spaced at one every 1/120 of a mile.)
definitions.example: Cedric claimed his old yacht could make 12 knots.

definitions.definition: A nautical mile
The bulbus glandis
In omegaverse fiction, a bulbus glandis-like structure on the penis of a male alpha, which ties him to an omega during intercourse.
To form into a knot; to tie with a knot or knots.
definitions.example: We knotted the ends of the rope to keep it from unravelling.

definitions.definition: To form wrinkles in the forehead, as a sign of concentration, concern, surprise, etc.
definitions.example: She knotted her brow in concentration while attempting to unravel the tangled strands.

definitions.definition: To unite closely; to knit together.
To entangle or perplex; to puzzle.
To form knots.
To knit knots for a fringe.
One of a variety of shore birds; the red-breasted sandpiper (variously Calidris canutus or Tringa canutus).
A bend; turn; curve; curvature; a flexure.
definitions.example: She held the baby in the crook of her arm.

definitions.definition: A bending of the knee; a genuflection.
A bent or curved part; a curving piece or portion (of anything).
definitions.example: the crook of a cane

definitions.definition: A lock or curl of hair.
A gibbet.
A support beam consisting of a post with a cross-beam resting upon it; a bracket or truss consisting of a vertical piece, a horizontal piece, and a strut.
A shepherd's crook; a staff with a semi-circular bend ("hook") at one end used by shepherds.
A bishop's staff of office.
An artifice; a trick; a contrivance.
A person who steals, lies, cheats or does other dishonest or illegal things; a criminal.
A pothook.
A small tube, usually curved, applied to a trumpet, horn, etc., to change its pitch or key.
To bend, or form into a hook.
definitions.example: He crooked his finger toward me.

definitions.definition: To become bent or hooked.
To turn from the path of rectitude; to pervert; to misapply; to twist.
(usually followed by of) An excessive amount or number; an abundance.
definitions.example: The menu offers a plethora of cuisines from around the world.

definitions.definition: An excess of red blood cells or bodily humours.
The spoils of war.
The act of pillaging.
To loot or plunder by force, especially in time of war.
A chief or superior.
Supreme; highest; chief.
Of the highest importance.
definitions.example: Getting those credit cards paid off is paramount.

definitions.definition: Association football
To kick the football directly off the ground, without using one's hands.
Of a student, to fail a class; to not pass.
definitions.example: He flunked math, again.

definitions.definition: Of a teacher, to deny a student a passing grade.
definitions.example: Unsatisfied with Fred's progress, the teacher flunked him.

definitions.definition: To shirk (a task or duty).
To back out through fear. (Commonly in the phrase 'flunk it', the 'it' referring to a specific task avoided; sometimes without specific reference describing a person's attitude to life in general.)
To take (a system, etc.) offline; to demote from an active or online state.

Of a system, currently not connected (generally electrically) to a larger network. For example, a power plant which is not connected to the grid, or a computer which is not connected to the Internet or to any other communications service.
definitions.example: I saved the web pages so I could read them later offline.

definitions.definition: (by extension) Happening in the physical world (the real world) as opposed to on the internet.

definitions.definition: While offline; with an offline system.
Outside the current meeting, in a more private setting.
A tract of land in which the dead are buried.
(by extension) A final storage place for collections of things that are no longer useful or useable.
To completely remove, get rid of, put an end to.
To kill (a person or animal).
definitions.example: a ruthless mobster who eliminated his enemies

definitions.definition: To excrete (waste products).
To exclude (from investigation or from further competition).
definitions.example: Bill was eliminated as a suspect when the police interviewed witnesses.

definitions.definition: To record amounts in a consolidation statement to remove the effects of inter-company transactions.
To have a nap; to sleep for a short period of time, especially during the day.
To be off one's guard.
definitions.example: The regulators were caught napping by the financial collapse.

definitions.definition: To form or raise a soft or fuzzy surface on (fabric or leather).
To grab; to nab.
To cover (something) with a sauce (usually in passive).
The act of taking a nap or short sleep.
The act or process of raising a nap, as on cloth.
Any serious injury to the body, often resulting from violence or an accident.
An emotional wound leading to psychological injury.
An event that causes great distress.
A supernatural being of varying size, now especially a grotesque humanoid creature living in caves or hills or under bridges.
An ugly person of either sex, especially one seeking sexual experiences.

Optical ejections from the top of the electrically active core regions of thunderstorms that are red in color that seem to occur after tendrils of vigorous sprites extend downward toward the cloud tops.
An instance of trolling, especially, in fishing, the trailing of a baited line.
A person who provokes others (chiefly on the Internet) for their own personal amusement or to cause disruption.
To saunter.
To trundle, to roll from side to side.
To draw someone or something out, to entice, to lure as if with trailing bait.
(by extension) To fish using a line and bait or lures trailed behind a boat similarly to trawling; to lure fish with bait.
To angle for with a trolling line, or with a hook drawn along the surface of the water; hence, to allure.
To fish in; to try to catch fish from.
To stroll about in order to find a sexual partner.
definitions.example: He spends most of his waking hours trolling on WIRE.

definitions.definition: (to post inflammatory material so as) to attempt to lure others into combative argument for purposes of personal entertainment and/or gratuitous disruption, especially in an online community or discussion
(by extension) To incite anger (including outside of an Internet context); to provoke, harass or annoy.
The act of moving round; routine; repetition.
A song whose parts are sung in succession; a catch; a round.
A trolley.
To move circularly; to roll; to turn.
To send about; to circulate, as a vessel in drinking.
To sing the parts of in succession, as of a round, a catch, and the like; also, to sing loudly, freely or in a carefree way.
A device for grasping an object and an attached means of moving it, as a rope and hook.
A block and tackle.
Equipment (rod, reel, line, lure, etc.) used when angling.
(by extension) equipment, gear, gadgetry.
A play where a player attempts to take control over the ball from an opponent, as in rugby or football.
A play where a defender brings the ball carrier to the ground.
Any instance in which one person intercepts another and forces them to the ground.
An offensive line position between a guard and an end: offensive tackle; a person playing that position.
A defensive position between two defensive ends: defensive tackle; a person playing that position.
A man's genitalia.
To force a person to the ground with the weight of one's own body, usually by jumping on top or slamming one's weight into him or her.
To face or deal with, attempting to overcome or fight down.
definitions.example: The government's measures to tackle crime were insufficient.

definitions.definition: To attempt to take away a ball.
To bring a ball carrier to the ground.
To "hit on" or pursue a person that one is interested in.
A doctrine held by a member of a religion at variance with established religious beliefs, especially dissension from Roman Catholic dogma.
A controversial or unorthodox opinion held by a member of a group, as in politics, philosophy or science.
To undo or open a lock or something locked by, for example, turning a key, or selecting a combination.
definitions.example: I unlocked the door and walked in.

definitions.definition: To obtain access to something.
definitions.example: I unlocked the dictionary article so I could edit it.

definitions.definition: To disclose or reveal previously unknown knowledge.
definitions.example: The discovery of a clue unlocked the mystery.

definitions.definition: To be or become unfastened or unrestrained.
The act by which something is unlocked.
A submicroscopic, non-cellular structure consisting of a core of DNA or RNA surrounded by a protein coat, that requires a living host cell to replicate, and often causes disease in the host organism.
A disease caused by these organisms.
definitions.example: He caught a virus and had to stay home from school.

definitions.definition: Venom, as produced by a poisonous animal etc.

A type of malware which can covertly transmit itself between computers via networks (especially the Internet) or removable storage such as disks, often causing damage to systems and data; also computer virus.
Any type of malware.
To hit with a whip.
definitions.example: The rider whipped the horse.

definitions.definition: (by extension) To hit with any flexible object.
definitions.example: I whipped her with a newspaper.

definitions.definition: To defeat, as in a contest or game.
To mix in a rapid aerating fashion, especially food.
definitions.example: to whip eggs or cream

definitions.definition: To urge into action or obedience.
definitions.example: He whipped the department into shape.

definitions.definition: To enforce a member voting in accordance with party policy.
To bind the end of a rope with twine or other small stuff to prevent its unlaying: fraying or unravelling.
To hoist or purchase by means of a whip.
To sew lightly; specifically, to form (a fabric) into gathers by loosely overcasting the rolled edge and drawing up the thread.
definitions.example: to whip a ruffle

definitions.definition: To throw or kick an object at a high velocity.
definitions.example: He whipped the ball at me.

definitions.definition: To fish a body of water especially by making repeated casts.
To snap back and forth like a whip.
definitions.example: The pennants whipped in the wind.

definitions.definition: To move very fast.
definitions.example: The wind whipped through the valley.

definitions.definition: To move (something) very fast; often with up, out, etc.
To transfer momentum from one skater to another.
To lash with sarcasm, abuse, etc.
To thrash; to beat out, as grain, by striking.
definitions.example: to whip wheat

definitions.definition: Controlled by a spouse or significant other to an unreasonable degree.
definitions.example: He knew he was whipped when he turned down a football game so they could shop for linens.

definitions.definition: To clean with water.
definitions.example: Dishwashers wash dishes much more efficiently than most humans.

definitions.definition: To move or erode by the force of water in motion.
definitions.example: Heavy rains wash a road or an embankment.

definitions.definition: To separate valuable material (such as gold) from worthless material by the action of flowing water.
To clean oneself with water.
definitions.example: I wash every morning after getting up.

definitions.definition: To cover with water or any liquid; to wet; to fall on and moisten.
definitions.example: Waves wash the shore.

definitions.definition: To move with a lapping or swashing sound; to lap or splash.
definitions.example: to hear the water washing

definitions.definition: To be eroded or carried away by the action of water.
To be cogent, convincing; to withstand critique.
To bear without injury the operation of being washed.
definitions.example: Some calicoes do not wash.

definitions.definition: To be wasted or worn away by the action of water, as by a running or overflowing stream, or by the dashing of the sea; said of road, a beach, etc.
To cover with a thin or watery coat of colour; to tint lightly and thinly.
To overlay with a thin coat of metal.
definitions.example: steel washed with silver

definitions.definition: To cause dephosphorization of (molten pig iron) by adding substances containing iron oxide, and sometimes manganese oxide.
To pass (a gas or gaseous mixture) through or over a liquid for the purpose of purifying it, especially by removing soluble constituents.
The action of the verb to wash
Clothing, bedlinen or soft furnishings that have been, are currently being, or are to be washed; laundry.
definitions.example: My mother used to do the washing on a Monday

definitions.definition: (often in the plural) The residue after an ore, etc, has been washed
definitions.example: The washings have a higher concentration of metal

definitions.definition: The liquid used to wash an ore.
A place where a precious metal found in gravel is separated from lighter material by washing.
definitions.example: the gold-washings, or silver-washings

definitions.definition: A thin covering or coat.
definitions.example: a washing of silver

definitions.definition: A fraudulent transaction in which the same stock is simultaneously bought and sold for the purpose of manipulating the market.
The covering of a piece with an infusible powder, which prevents it from sticking to its supports, while receiving the glaze.
